text:
**F3 — audit explanations are latest-per-key and ephemeral.** `record_transaction_audit` overwrites `audit.node_changes` / `resource_commands` / `output_frames` per key every commit and clears them when audit is Disabled (`audit.rs:52-146`, `audit_types.rs:16-24`). `why_changed`/`why_resource_command` answer *"why did this key LAST change,"* nothing historical. Historical "why" must come from persisted commits + replay, not a live query. This bounds `probe why` and the acid test.
 [...] - **The value numbers are partly modeled.** The old five-trigger status path is deleted; "publishes avoided" compares against a reconstructed baseline. Label it in the output, always. Similarly, replay fidelity depends on the drive log capturing *every* seam call in order — the three graphs are mutually independent (per-surface ordering suffices), but a missed call site silently breaks trace equality; the seams ratchet is also the record-completeness ratchet.
- **Explanations have bounded retention in core** — audit state is latest-per-key (`AuditState` clears per transaction). `probe why` answers about the *most recent* command/frame per key; history lives only in persisted receipts. The UX must say "latest" explicitly, or users will read a fresh explanation as covering last Tuesday.
